against disease in the first generation offspring in mice.

The study, published in the journal Science, was conducted by a team of
scientists led by Dr. Michael D. Mann, a professor of meteorology at
the University of Massachusetts, Amherst.

"We have been able to show that the environmental factors that affect
the development of asthma in the first generation offspring of
rats are the same factors that affect the development of asthma in
human beings," said Dr. Mann. "This is a very important finding, because
it means that environmental factors that affect asthma in humans
can also affect asthma in rats."

The study was conducted in the laboratory of Dr. Mann, who is
a member of the National Academy of Sciences.

The study was funded by the National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences
and the National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences/Environmental
Health Sciences Research and Development Program.
